title: Rich, Buttery Polish Shrimp Fondue
description: Comforting, dense, and flavorful, this Polish-inspired fondue is a perfect vessel for a satisfying meal with friends. Featuring shrimp stock, lardons-render and a hint of earthy mushrooms.

ingredients.name: Lardons
ingredients.notes: small pieces of bacon or pancetta

ingredients.name: Mushrooms
ingredients.notes: sliced, such as button or cremini

instructions: Heat the lardons in a small skillet over medium heat until crispy, about 5 minutes.
Remove the lardons from the skillet with a slotted spoon and set aside.
Add the diced onion to the skillet and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the sliced mushrooms to the skillet and cook until they release their moisture and start to brown, about 4-5 minutes.
Add the shrimp to the skillet and cook until pink and just cooked through, about 2-3 minutes.
Add the shrimp stock to the skillet and bring to a simmer.
Stir in the softened butter until melted.
If using flour, stir to combine and cook for 1-2 minutes, until lightly thickened.
Transfer the fondue to a fondue pot and serve immediately, garnished with the reserved lardons and a sprinkle of parsley, if desired.
